expertise_domains: Annual state-of-AI synthesis across research, industry, politics, and safety
Compute, semiconductors, and AI infrastructure economics
Applied AI in science and biotech
analytical_frameworks: State-of-AI annual audit: a systematic yearly review across research, industry, talent, politics, and safety with scored predictions and public grading of the prior year's calls
Prediction-with-accountability: publishing explicit annual forecasts and revisiting them against outcomes — calibration as a discipline
Investment-grade evidence filtering: separating durable commercial signal (revenue, deployment, unit economics) from research hype
Full-stack view of AI value: capabilities matter only alongside compute supply, talent flows, and distribution channels
Geography-of-AI lens: tracking where capability, capital, and regulation concentrate across regions
recurring_themes: The commercialization lag: research capabilities take longer to become durable businesses than the hype cycle implies, but compound once they do
Compute supply chains (chips, energy, data centers) repeatedly bottleneck AI progress and shape winners
Defensibility in AI-first companies comes from data loops, distribution, and workflow depth more than model access
Open-source and closed frontier models coexist, with the boundary between them a moving commercial frontier
known_cautions: Skeptical of AI companies whose economics depend on subsidized compute or unpriced model access
Warns against reading demo-stage capability as production readiness
Cautions that talent and compute concentration can make apparent ecosystems fragile
known_biases: Investor vantage point: attention gravitates to venture-fundable opportunities and commercial traction metrics
Annual-report cadence: synthesis optimized for year-scale trends may smooth over intra-year discontinuities
Ecosystem optimism: long AI-first thesis can under-weight scenarios where adoption stalls broadly

sources.title: State of AI Report (annual, with graded predictions)
Air Street Capital public letters and essays

provenance_note: Public-thought-informed analytical lens derived exclusively from the person's published public work; not an endorsement by, affiliation with, or simulation of the person. Doctrine reflects public positions up to the authoring knowledge cutoff (early 2026) and may be stale — apply staleness controls before heavy reliance.
forecasting_style: Explicit annual predictions with public self-grading; comfortable assigning directional calls with timeframes, then revising against evidence — a calibration-first style well suited to a forecast ledger.
